NKJV.1: Rejoice in the LORD, O you righteous! For praise from the upright is beautiful.
NKJV.2: Praise the LORD with the harp; Make melody to Him with an instrument of ten strings.
NKJV.3: Sing to Him a new song; Play skillfully with a shout of joy.
NKJV.4: For the word of the LORD is right, And all His work is done in truth.
NKJV.5: He loves righteousness and justice; The earth is full of the goodness of the LORD.
NKJV.6: By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, And all the host of them by the breath of His mouth.
NKJV.7: He gathers the waters of the sea together as a heap; He lays up the deep in storehouses.
NKJV.8: Let all the earth fear the LORD; Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
NKJV.9: For He spoke, and it was done; He commanded, and it stood fast.
NKJV.10: The LORD brings the counsel of the nations to nothing; He makes the plans of the peoples of no effect.
NKJV.11: The counsel of the LORD stands forever, The plans of His heart to all generations.
NKJV.12: Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, The people He has chosen as His own inheritance.
NKJV.13: The LORD looks from heaven; He sees all the sons of men.
NKJV.14: From the place of His dwelling He looks On all the inhabitants of the earth;
NKJV.15: He fashions their hearts individually;  He considers all their works.
NKJV.16: No king is saved by the multitude of an army; A mighty man is not delivered by great strength.
NKJV.17: A horse is a vain hope for safety; Neither shall it deliver any by its great strength.
NKJV.18: Behold, the eye of the LORD is on those who fear Him, On those who hope in His mercy,
NKJV.19: To deliver their soul from death, And to keep them alive in famine.
NKJV.20: Our soul waits for the LORD; He is our help and our shield.
NKJV.21: For our heart shall rejoice in Him, Because we have trusted in His holy name.
NKJV.22: Let Your mercy, O LORD, be upon us, Just as we hope in You.

KJV.1: Rejoice<S>7442</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>, O ye righteous<S>6662</S>: for praise<S>8416</S> is comely<S>5000</S> for the upright<S>3477</S>.

KJV.11: The counsel<S>6098</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> standeth<S>5975</S> for ever<S>5769</S>, the thoughts<S>4284</S> of his heart<S>3820</S> to all<S>1755</S> generations<S>1755</S>. <sup>to all: Heb. to generation and generation</sup>
KJV.12: Blessed<S>835</S> is the nation<S>1471</S> whose God<S>430</S> is the LORD<S>3068</S>; and the people<S>5971</S> whom he hath chosen<S>977</S> for his own inheritance<S>5159</S>.
KJV.13: The LORD<S>3068</S> looketh<S>5027</S> from heaven<S>8064</S>; he beholdeth<S>7200</S> all the sons<S>1121</S> of men<S>120</S>.
KJV.14: From the place<S>4349</S> of his habitation<S>3427</S> he looketh<S>7688</S> upon all the inhabitants<S>3427</S> of the earth<S>776</S>.
KJV.15: He fashioneth<S>3335</S> their hearts<S>3820</S> alike<S>3162</S>; he considereth<S>995</S> all their works<S>4639</S>.
KJV.16: There is no king<S>4428</S> saved<S>3467</S> by the multitude<S>7230</S> of an host<S>2428</S>: a mighty man<S>1368</S> is not delivered<S>5337</S> by much<S>7230</S> strength<S>3581</S>.
KJV.17: An horse<S>5483</S> is a vain thing<S>8267</S> for safety<S>8668</S>: neither shall he deliver<S>4422</S> any by his great<S>7230</S> strength<S>2428</S>.

ESV.1: Shout for joy in the LORD, O you righteous! Praise befits the upright.
ESV.2: Give thanks to the LORD with the lyre; make melody to him with the harp of ten strings!
ESV.3: Sing to him a new song; play skillfully on the strings, with loud shouts.
ESV.4: For the word of the LORD is upright, and all his work is done in faithfulness.
ESV.5: He loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of the steadfast love of the LORD.
ESV.6: By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, and by the breath of his mouth all their host.
ESV.7: He gathers the waters of the sea as a heap; he puts the deeps in storehouses.
ESV.8: Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him!
ESV.9: For he spoke, and it came to be; he commanded, and it stood firm.
ESV.10: The LORD brings the counsel of the nations to nothing; he frustrates the plans of the peoples.
ESV.11: The counsel of the LORD stands forever, the plans of his heart to all generations.
ESV.12: Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, the people whom he has chosen as his heritage!
ESV.13: The LORD looks down from heaven; he sees all the children of man;
ESV.14: from where he sits enthroned he looks out on all the inhabitants of the earth,
ESV.15: he who fashions the hearts of them all and observes all their deeds.
ESV.16: The king is not saved by his great army; a warrior is not delivered by his great strength.
ESV.17: The war horse is a false hope for salvation, and by its great might it cannot rescue.
ESV.18: Behold, the eye of the LORD is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his steadfast love,
ESV.19: that he may deliver their soul from death and keep them alive in famine.
ESV.20: Our soul waits for the LORD; he is our help and our shield.
ESV.21: For our heart is glad in him, because we trust in his holy name.
ESV.22: Let your steadfast love, O LORD, be upon us, even as we hope in you.

NASB.1: Sing for joy in the LORD, O you righteous ones; Praise is becoming to the upright.
NASB.2: Give thanks to the LORD with the lyre; Sing praises to Him with a harp of ten strings.
NASB.3: Sing to Him a new song; Play skillfully with a shout of joy.
NASB.4: For the word of the LORD is upright, And all His work is [done] in faithfulness.
NASB.5: He loves righteousness and justice; The earth is full of the lovingkindness of the LORD.
NASB.6: By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, And by the breath of His mouth all their host.
NASB.7: He gathers the waters of the sea together as a heap; He lays up the deeps in storehouses.
NASB.8: Let all the earth fear the LORD; Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
NASB.9: For He spoke, and it was done; He commanded, and it stood fast.
NASB.10: The LORD nullifies the counsel of the nations; He frustrates the plans of the peoples.
NASB.11: The counsel of the LORD stands forever, The plans of His heart from generation to generation.
NASB.12: Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, The people whom He has chosen for His own inheritance.
NASB.13: The LORD looks from heaven; He sees all the sons of men;
NASB.14: From His dwelling place He looks out On all the inhabitants of the earth,
NASB.15: He who fashions the hearts of them all, He who understands all their works.
NASB.16: The king is not saved by a mighty army; A warrior is not delivered by great strength.
NASB.17: A horse is a false hope for victory; Nor does it deliver anyone by its great strength.
NASB.18: Behold, the eye of the LORD is on those who fear Him, On those who hope for His lovingkindness,
NASB.19: To deliver their soul from death And to keep them alive in famine.
NASB.20: Our soul waits for the LORD; He is our help and our shield.
NASB.21: For our heart rejoices in Him, Because we trust in His holy name.
NASB.22: Let Your lovingkindness, O LORD, be upon us, According as we have hoped in You.

NLT.1:
Let the godly sing for joy to the LORD;
it is fitting for the pure to praise him.
NLT.2:
Praise the LORD with melodies on the lyre;
make music for him on the ten-stringed harp.
NLT.3:
Sing a new song of praise to him;
play skillfully on the harp, and sing with joy.
NLT.4:
For the word of the LORD holds true,
and we can trust everything he does.
NLT.5:
He loves whatever is just and good;
the unfailing love of the LORD fills the earth.
NLT.6:
The LORD merely spoke,
and the heavens were created.
He breathed the word,
and all the stars were born.
NLT.7:
He assigned the sea its boundaries
and locked the oceans in vast reservoirs.
NLT.8:
Let the whole world fear the LORD,
and let everyone stand in awe of him.
NLT.9:
For when he spoke, the world began!
It appeared at his command.
NLT.10:
The LORD frustrates the plans of the nations
and thwarts all their schemes.
NLT.11:
But the LORD’s plans stand firm forever;
his intentions can never be shaken.
NLT.12:
What joy for the nation whose God is the LORD,
whose people he has chosen as his inheritance.
NLT.13:
The LORD looks down from heaven
and sees the whole human race.
NLT.14:
From his throne he observes
all who live on the earth.
NLT.15:
He made their hearts,
so he understands everything they do.
NLT.16:
The best-equipped army cannot save a king,
nor is great strength enough to save a warrior.
NLT.17:
Don’t count on your warhorse to give you victory —
for all its strength, it cannot save you.
NLT.18:
But the LORD watches over those who fear him,
those who rely on his unfailing love.
NLT.19:
He rescues them from death
and keeps them alive in times of famine.
NLT.20:
We put our hope in the LORD.
He is our help and our shield.
NLT.21:
In him our hearts rejoice,
for we trust in his holy name.
NLT.22:
Let your unfailing love surround us, LORD,
for our hope is in you alone.

WEB.1: Rejoice in Yahweh, you righteous! Praise is fitting for the upright.
WEB.2: Give thanks to Yahweh with the lyre. Sing praises to him with the harp of ten strings.
WEB.3: Sing to him a new song. Play skillfully with a shout of joy!
WEB.4: For Yahweh’s word is right. All his work is done in faithfulness.
WEB.5: He loves righteousness and justice. The earth is full of the loving kindness of Yahweh.
WEB.6: By Yahweh’s word, the heavens were made; all their army by the breath of his mouth.
WEB.7: He gathers the waters of the sea together as a heap. He lays up the deeps in storehouses.
WEB.8: Let all the earth fear Yahweh. Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
WEB.9: For he spoke, and it was done. He commanded, and it stood firm.
WEB.10: Yahweh brings the counsel of the nations to nothing. He makes the thoughts of the peoples to be of no effect.
WEB.11: The counsel of Yahweh stands fast forever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
WEB.12: Blessed is the nation whose God is Yahweh, the people whom he has chosen for his own inheritance.
WEB.13: Yahweh looks from heaven. He sees all the sons of men.
WEB.14: From the place of his habitation he looks out on all the inhabitants of the earth,
WEB.15: he who fashions all of their hearts; and he considers all of their works.
WEB.16: There is no king saved by the multitude of an army. A mighty man is not delivered by great strength.
WEB.17: A horse is a vain thing for safety, neither does he deliver any by his great power.
WEB.18: Behold, Yahweh’s eye is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his loving kindness;
WEB.19: to deliver their soul from death, to keep them alive in famine.
WEB.20: Our soul has waited for Yahweh. He is our help and our shield.
WEB.21: For our heart rejoices in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.
WEB.22: Let your loving kindness be on us, Yahweh, since we have hoped in you.

CSB17.1:
Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous ones;
praise from the upright is beautiful. ⓝ
CSB17.2:
Praise the LORD with the lyre;
make music to him with a ten-stringed harp. ⓐ
CSB17.3:
Sing a new song to him; ⓑ
play skillfully on the strings, with a joyful shout. ⓒ
CSB17.4:
For the word of the LORD is right,
and all his work is trustworthy. ⓓ
CSB17.5:
He loves righteousness and justice; ⓔ
the earth is full of the LORD’s unfailing love. ⓕ
CSB17.6:
The heavens were made by the word of the LORD, ⓖ
and all the stars, by the breath of his mouth. ⓗ
CSB17.7:
He gathers the water of the sea into a heap; [1]
he puts the depths into storehouses. ⓘ
CSB17.8:
Let the whole earth fear the LORD;
let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him. ⓙ
CSB17.9:
For he spoke, and it came into being;
he commanded, and it came into existence. ⓚ
CSB17.10:
The LORD frustrates the counsel of the nations;
he thwarts the plans of the peoples. ⓛ
CSB17.11:
The counsel of the LORD stands forever,
the plans of his heart from generation to generation. ⓜ
CSB17.12:
Happy is the nation whose God is the LORD  —
the people he has chosen to be his own possession! ⓝ
CSB17.13:
The LORD looks down from heaven;
he observes everyone. ⓐ
CSB17.14:
He gazes on all the inhabitants of the earth
from his dwelling place. ⓑ
CSB17.15:
He forms the hearts of them all;
he considers all their works. ⓒ
CSB17.16:
A king is not saved by a large army;
a warrior will not be rescued by great strength. ⓓ
CSB17.17:
The horse is a false hope for safety;
it provides no escape by its great power. ⓔ
CSB17.18:
But look, the LORD keeps his eye on those who fear him —
those who depend on his faithful love ⓕ
CSB17.19:
to rescue them from death
and to keep them alive in famine. ⓖ
CSB17.20:
We wait for the LORD; ⓗ
he is our help and shield. ⓘ
CSB17.21:
For our hearts rejoice in him ⓙ
because we trust in his holy name. ⓚ
CSB17.22:
May your faithful love rest on us, LORD,
for we put our hope in you. ⓛ

AMP.1:
Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous ones;
Praise is becoming and appropriate for those who are upright [in heart—those with moral integrity and godly character].
AMP.2:
Give thanks to the LORD with the lyre;
Sing praises to Him with the harp of ten strings.
AMP.3:
Sing to Him a new song;
Play skillfully [on the strings] with a loud and joyful sound.
AMP.4:
For the word of the LORD is right;
And all His work is done in faithfulness.
AMP.5:
He loves righteousness and justice;
The earth is full of the lovingkindness of the LORD.
AMP.6:
By the word of the LORD were the heavens made,
And all their host by the breath of His mouth.
AMP.7:
He gathers the waters of the sea together as in a wineskin;
He puts the deeps in storehouses.
AMP.8:
Let all the earth fear and worship the LORD;
Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
AMP.9:
For He spoke, and it was done;
He commanded, and it stood fast.
AMP.10:
The LORD nullifies the counsel of the nations;
He makes the thoughts and plans of the people ineffective.
AMP.11:
The counsel of the LORD stands forever,
The thoughts and plans of His heart through all generations.
AMP.12:
Blessed [fortunate, prosperous, and favored by God] is the nation whose God is the LORD,
The people whom He has chosen as His own inheritance.
AMP.13:
The LORD looks [down] from heaven;
He sees all the sons of man;
AMP.14:
From His dwelling place He looks closely
Upon all the inhabitants of the earth—
AMP.15:
He who fashions the hearts of them all,
Who considers and understands all that they do.
AMP.16:
The king is not saved by the great size of his army;
A warrior is not rescued by his great strength.
AMP.17:
A horse is a false hope for victory;
Nor does it deliver anyone by its great strength.
AMP.18:
Behold, the eye of the LORD is upon those who fear Him [and worship Him with awe-inspired reverence and obedience],
On those who hope [confidently] in His compassion and lovingkindness,
AMP.19:
To rescue their lives from death
And keep them alive in famine.
AMP.20:
We wait [expectantly] for the LORD;
He is our help and our shield.
AMP.21:
For in Him our heart rejoices,
Because we trust [lean on, rely on, and are confident] in His holy name.
AMP.22:
Let Your [steadfast] lovingkindness, O LORD, be upon us,
In proportion as we have hoped in You.

MSG.1:
Good people, cheer God!
Right-living people sound best when praising.
MSG.2:
Use guitars to reinforce your Hallelujahs!
Play his praise on a grand piano!
MSG.3:
Invent your own new song to him;
give him a trumpet fanfare.
MSG.4:
For God's Word is solid to the core;
everything he makes is sound inside and out.
MSG.5:
He loves it when everything fits,
when his world is in plumb-line true.
Earth is drenched
in God's affectionate satisfaction.
MSG.6:
The skies were made by God's command;
he breathed the word and the stars popped out.
MSG.7:
He scooped Sea into his jug,
put Ocean in his keg.
MSG.8:
Earth-creatures, bow before God;
world-dwellers—down on your knees!
MSG.9:
Here's why: he spoke and there it was,
in place the moment he said so.
MSG.10:
God takes the wind out of Babel pretense,
he shoots down the world's power-schemes.
MSG.11:
God's plan for the world stands up,
all his designs are made to last.
MSG.12:
Blessed is the country with God for God;
blessed are the people he's put in his will.
MSG.13:
From high in the skies God looks around,
he sees all Adam's brood.
MSG.14:
From where he sits
he overlooks all us earth-dwellers.
MSG.15:
He has shaped each person in turn;
now he watches everything we do.
MSG.16:
No king succeeds with a big army alone,
no warrior wins by brute strength.
MSG.17:
Horsepower is not the answer;
no one gets by on muscle alone.
MSG.18:
Watch this: God's eye is on those who respect him,
the ones who are looking for his love.
MSG.19:
He's ready to come to their rescue in bad times;
in lean times he keeps body and soul together.
MSG.20:
We're depending on God;
he's everything we need.
MSG.21:
What's more, our hearts brim with joy
since we've taken for our own his holy name.
MSG.22:
Love us, God, with all you've got—
that's what we're depending on.

NIV.1:
Psalm 33
Sing joyfully to the Lord, you righteous;
it is fitting for the upright to praise him.
NIV.2:
Praise the Lord with the harp;
make music to him on the ten-stringed lyre.
NIV.3:
Sing to him a new song;
play skillfully, and shout for joy.
NIV.4:
For the word of the Lord is right and true;
he is faithful in all he does.
NIV.5:
The Lord loves righteousness and justice;
the earth is full of his unfailing love.
NIV.6:
By the word of the Lord were the heavens made,
their starry host by the breath of his mouth.
NIV.7:
He gathers the waters of the sea into jars;
he puts the deep into storehouses.
NIV.8:
Let all the earth fear the Lord;
let all the people of the world revere him.
NIV.9:
For he spoke, and it came to be;
he commanded, and it stood firm.
NIV.10:
The Lord foils the plans of the nations;
he thwarts the purposes of the peoples.
NIV.11:
But the plans of the Lord stand firm forever,
the purposes of his heart through all generations.
NIV.12:
Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord,
the people he chose for his inheritance.
NIV.13:
From heaven the Lord looks down
and sees all mankind;
NIV.14:
from his dwelling place he watches
all who live on earth—
NIV.15:
he who forms the hearts of all,
who considers everything they do.
NIV.16:
No king is saved by the size of his army;
no warrior escapes by his great strength.
NIV.17:
A horse is a vain hope for deliverance;
despite all its great strength it cannot save.
NIV.18:
But the eyes of the Lord are on those who fear him,
on those whose hope is in his unfailing love,
NIV.19:
to deliver them from death
and keep them alive in famine.
NIV.20:
We wait in hope for the Lord;
he is our help and our shield.
NIV.21:
In him our hearts rejoice,
for we trust in his holy name.
NIV.22:
May your unfailing love rest upon us, O Lord,
even as we put our hope in you.

NRSVCE.1:
Rejoice in the LORD, O you righteous.
Praise befits the upright.
NRSVCE.2:
Praise the LORD with the lyre;
make melody to him with the harp of ten strings.
NRSVCE.3:
Sing to him a new song;
play skillfully on the strings, with loud shouts.
NRSVCE.4:
For the word of the LORD is upright,
and all his work is done in faithfulness.
NRSVCE.5:
He loves righteousness and justice;
the earth is full of the steadfast love of the LORD.
NRSVCE.6:
By the word of the LORD the heavens were made,
and all their host by the breath of his mouth.
NRSVCE.7:
He gathered the waters of the sea as in a bottle;
he put the deeps in storehouses.
NRSVCE.8:
Let all the earth fear the LORD;
let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
NRSVCE.9:
For he spoke, and it came to be;
he commanded, and it stood firm.
NRSVCE.10:
The LORD brings the counsel of the nations to nothing;
he frustrates the plans of the peoples.
NRSVCE.11:
The counsel of the LORD stands forever,
the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
NRSVCE.12:
Happy is the nation whose God is the LORD,
the people whom he has chosen as his heritage.
NRSVCE.13:
The LORD looks down from heaven;
he sees all humankind.
NRSVCE.14:
From where he sits enthroned he watches
all the inhabitants of the earth —
NRSVCE.15:
he who fashions the hearts of them all,
and observes all their deeds.
NRSVCE.16:
A king is not saved by his great army;
a warrior is not delivered by his great strength.
NRSVCE.17:
The war horse is a vain hope for victory,
and by its great might it cannot save.
NRSVCE.18:
Truly the eye of the LORD is on those who fear him,
on those who hope in his steadfast love,
NRSVCE.19:
to deliver their soul from death,
and to keep them alive in famine.
NRSVCE.20:
Our soul waits for the LORD;
he is our help and shield.
NRSVCE.21:
Our heart is glad in him,
because we trust in his holy name.
NRSVCE.22:
Let your steadfast love, O LORD, be upon us,
even as we hope in you.

NET.1: You godly ones, shout for joy because of the LORD! It is appropriate for the morally upright to offer him praise.
NET.2: Give thanks to the LORD with the harp! Sing to him to the accompaniment of a ten-stringed instrument!
NET.3: Sing to him a new song! Play skillfully as you shout out your praises to him!
NET.4: For the LORD’s decrees are just, and everything he does is fair.
NET.5: The LORD promotes equity and justice; the LORD’s faithfulness extends throughout the earth.
NET.6: By the LORD’s decree the heavens were made; by a mere word from his mouth all the stars in the sky were created.
NET.7: He piles up the water of the sea; he puts the oceans in storehouses.
NET.8: Let the whole earth fear the LORD! Let all who live in the world stand in awe of him!
NET.9: For he spoke, and it came into existence, he issued the decree, and it stood firm.
NET.10: The LORD frustrates the decisions of the nations; he nullifies the plans of the peoples.
NET.11: The LORD’s decisions stand forever; his plans abide throughout the ages.
NET.12: How blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, the people whom he has chosen to be his special possession.
NET.13: The LORD watches from heaven; he sees all people.
NET.14: From the place where he lives he looks carefully at all the earth’s inhabitants.
NET.15: He is the one who forms every human heart, and takes note of all their actions.
NET.16: No king is delivered by his vast army; a warrior is not saved by his great might.
NET.17: A horse disappoints those who trust in it for victory; despite its great strength, it cannot deliver.
NET.18: Look, the LORD takes notice of his loyal followers, those who wait for him to demonstrate his faithfulness
NET.19: by saving their lives from death and sustaining them during times of famine.
NET.20: We wait for the LORD; he is our deliverer and shield.
NET.21: For our hearts rejoice in him, for we trust in his holy name.
NET.22: May we experience your faithfulness, O LORD, for we wait for you.
